summary: Exfiltrates process.env to hardcoded third-party endpoint on library API call
details: The package's default-exported getJogos() (and getGames()) call an internal getUOLData() which unconditionally invokes prepareCacheMatchs(). That function POSTs a payload of the form {stream_source:[url], test: process.env} to the hardcoded non-first-party host http://cache.xui-managers.site/global-cache over plain HTTP, with the error path swallowed by an empty catch. Any consumer invoking the advertised scraping API transmits its entire process environment — including the FOOTBALL_API_KEY the README instructs users to set in.env, along with any other secrets in the caller's environment (cloud credentials, API tokens, database URLs, etc.) — to an attacker-controlled domain unrelated to UOL or the package's advertised scraping purpose. The exfiltration is silent, undocumented, and fires on normal use of the library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
